Details: Removal of Cryptosporidium in water by slow sand Ffltration can be very effective (>99%), where the effectiveness is dependent primarily on the source water quality and temperature. Cryptosporidium removal is highly dependent on water quality due to the chemical pretreatment in the process. Under cold water operating conditions, Cryptosporidium removal has not been found to be as effective, with removals averaging less than 50%. The slow sand filter media also impacts removal. LT1 cites slow sand filtration as an effective means of Cryptosporidium removal, provided that turbidity control is maintained.
